page: Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Env6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured as a memory-optimized and network-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base frequency of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong capability in single-threaded operations, particularly highlighted by a strong gzip compression score of 31.08 GB/sec. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores fall into the average tier. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3927.87 and a weak bzip3 multi-threaded decompression throughput of 25.75 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth benchmarks also sit in the lower tier, with uncached read and write speeds of 31.34 GB/sec and 23.07 GB/sec respectively.

From a workload perspective, the E4ns_v6 is suited for memory-intensive tasks that do not require heavy parallel multi-core execution or high-throughput local disk access. It performs adequately in static web serving, achieving a throughput of 7.48 GB/sec, and handles small-scale language model inference reasonably well, processing 472.46 tokens/sec for a 135M model prompt. However, it exhibits weak performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks, making it less optimal for high-throughput transactional databases. The instance offers a qualitative balance of high memory allocation per vCPU and dedicated high-frequency Intel compute, making it a cost-efficient option for network-optimized workloads requiring moderate processing power but substantial memory overhead.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is a memory-optimized and network-optimized virtual machine from the Env6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This x86_64 instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, such as 31.08 GB/sec in gzip compression, alongside average single-core CPU capabilities. However, multi-core processing and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. The instance is best suited for network-optimized workloads, static web serving, and memory-intensive applications with moderate parallel processing demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is a memory-optimized, network-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded compression performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized, network-ready virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel processors and dedicated resources for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-threaded performance with 31.08 GB/sec gzip compression
Network-optimized and premium storage capable with zero bundled local storage
Best suited for static web serving, small-scale LLM inference, and memory-heavy workloads
